tZh: AgentBench：评估作为智能体的大语言模型
sEn: AgentBench is a multi-dimensional benchmark with 8 environments to evaluate LLMs as agents, revealing significant performance gaps between top commercial and open-source models.
sZh: AgentBench是一个包含8个不同环境的多维基准，用于评估作为智能体的大语言模型，揭示了顶级商业模型与开源模型之间的显著性能差距。
absEn: The potential of Large Language Model (LLM) as agents has been widely acknowledged recently. Thus, there is an urgent need to quantitatively \textit{evaluate LLMs as agents} on challenging tasks in interactive environments. We present AgentBench, a multi-dimensional benchmark that consists of 8 distinct environments to assess LLM-as-Agent's reasoning and decision-making abilities. Our extensive test over \num API-based and open-sourced (OSS) LLMs shows that, while top commercial LLMs present a strong ability of acting as agents in complex environments, there is a significant disparity in performance between them and many OSS competitors that are no larger than 70B. We identify the typical reasons of failures in environments and LLMs, showing that poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, and instruction following abilities are the main obstacles for developing usable LLM agents. Improving instruction following and training on high quality multi-round alignment data could improve agent performance. And different from existing assumptions, training on code present ambivalent impacts on different agent tasks. Datasets, environments, and an integrated evaluation package for AgentBench are released at https://github.com/THUDM/AgentBench.
absZh: 大语言模型作为智能体的潜力近期得到了广泛认可。因此，迫切需要定量评估大语言模型在交互式环境中执行具有挑战性任务时的智能体能力。我们提出了AgentBench，一个多维基准，包含8个不同的环境，用于评估大语言模型作为智能体的推理和决策能力。我们对基于API和开源的多个大语言模型进行了广泛测试，结果表明，虽然顶级商业大语言模型在复杂环境中表现出强大的智能体能力，但它们与许多不超过70B参数的开源模型之间存在显著的性能差距。我们识别了环境和模型中的典型失败原因，表明长期推理、决策和指令遵循能力不足是开发可用大语言模型智能体的主要障碍。改进指令遵循能力并训练高质量的多轮对齐数据可以提升智能体性能。与现有假设不同，代码训练对不同智能体任务的影响存在矛盾。AgentBench的数据集、环境和集成评估包已在https://github.com/THUDM/AgentBench发布。

contrib.en: Introduces AgentBench, a multi-dimensional benchmark with 8 environments for evaluating LLMs as agents.
contrib.zh: 提出AgentBench，一个包含8个环境的多维度基准，用于评估作为智能体的大语言模型。

contrib.en: Evaluates 27 LLMs, revealing significant performance gap between top commercial and open-source models.
contrib.zh: 评估了27个大语言模型，揭示了顶级商业模型与开源模型之间的显著性能差距。

contrib.en: Identifies poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, and instruction following as main obstacles for LLM agents.
contrib.zh: 指出长期推理、决策和指令遵循能力不足是开发可用大语言模型智能体的主要障碍。

contrib.en: Shows code training and high-quality alignment data can improve agent performance.
contrib.zh: 表明代码训练和高质量对齐数据可以提升智能体性能。

contrib.en: Releases datasets, environments, and an integrated evaluation toolkit for AgentBench.
contrib.zh: 发布了AgentBench的数据集、环境和集成评估工具包。

limits.en: Only evaluates text-only LLMs, excluding multi-modal models.
limits.zh: 仅评估纯文本大语言模型，不包括多模态模型。

limits.en: Open-source LLMs evaluated are limited to those smaller than 70B parameters.
limits.zh: 评估的开源大语言模型仅限于参数小于70B的模型。

limits.en: Uses only basic Chain-of-Thought prompting, not advanced strategies like reflection or search.
limits.zh: 仅使用基本的思维链提示，未采用反思或搜索等高级策略。

limits.en: Evaluation may not fully reflect real-world deployment due to simplified environments.
limits.zh: 由于环境简化，评估可能无法完全反映实际部署情况。

limits.en: Score normalization method may obscure absolute performance differences across tasks.
limits.zh: 分数归一化方法可能掩盖任务间的绝对性能差异。

items.en: Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming increasingly smart and autonomous, targeting real-world pragmatic missions beyond traditional NLP tasks. As a result, there has been an urgent need to evaluate LLMs as agents on challenging tasks in interactive environments. We present AgentBench, a multi-dimensional evolving benchmark that currently consists of 8 distinct environments to assess LLM-as-Agent’s reasoning and decision-making abilities in a multi-turn open-ended generation setting. Our extensive test over 27 API-based and open-sourced (OSS) LLMs shows that, while top commercial LLMs present a strong ability of acting as agents in complex environments, there is a significant disparity in performance between them and OSS competitors. We identify the typical reasons of failures in environments and LLMs, showing that poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, and instruction following abilities are the main obstacles for developing usable LLM agents. Training on code and high quality multi-turn alignment data could improve agent performance. Datasets, environments, and an integrated evaluation package for AgentBench are released at https://github.com/THUDM/AgentBench.
items.zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）正变得越来越智能和自主，其目标已超越传统自然语言处理任务，转向现实世界中的实用任务。因此，迫切需要将LLM作为智能体，在交互式环境中评估其处理挑战性任务的能力。我们提出了AgentBench，一个多维度的演进式基准测试，目前包含8个不同的环境，用于评估LLM作为智能体在多轮开放式生成场景中的推理和决策能力。我们对27个基于API和开源（OSS）的LLM进行了广泛测试，结果显示，虽然顶级商业LLM在复杂环境中表现出强大的智能体能力，但它们与开源竞争对手之间存在显著的性能差距。我们识别了环境和LLM中典型的失败原因，表明长期推理、决策和指令遵循能力不足是开发可用LLM智能体的主要障碍。在代码和高质量多轮对齐数据上进行训练可以提升智能体性能。AgentBench的数据集、环境和集成评估包已在https://github.com/THUDM/AgentBench发布。

items.capEn: Figure 1: An overview of LLMs on AgentBench. While LLMs begin to manifest their proficiency in LLM-as-Agent, gaps between models and the distance toward practical usability are significant.
items.capZh: 图1：LLM在AgentBench上的概览。虽然LLM开始展现出作为智能体的能力，但模型之间的差距以及与实际可用性的距离仍然显著。

items.en: Intelligent agents and autonomous entities (Searle, 1970; Maes, 1994; Wooldridge & Jennings, 1995) that are capable of decision-making and action execution in particular environments have been key concepts of artificial intelligence (AI) historically. Notwithstanding substantial advancements in deep learning algorithms applied in both computer vision and natural language processing (NLP), their potential for developing efficient and practically usable assisting agents remains largely unexplored.
items.zh: 智能体和自主实体（Searle, 1970; Maes, 1994; Wooldridge & Jennings, 1995）能够在特定环境中进行决策和行动执行，历来是人工智能（AI）的关键概念。尽管深度学习算法在计算机视觉和自然语言处理（NLP）方面取得了显著进展，但其在开发高效且实用的辅助智能体方面的潜力仍未得到充分探索。

items.en: The advent of Large Language Models (LLMs) (Brown et al., 2020; Chowdhery et al., 2022; Touvron et al., 2023), such as GPT-4 (OpenAI, 2023), has brought plenty of new opportunities to this realm. Through extensive alignment training (Ouyang et al., 2022; Wei et al., 2022a; Sanh et al., 2022), LLMs have not only mastered traditional NLP tasks but also showcased an impressive ability to comprehend human intent and execute instructions. This has spurred the development of various LLM-based applications for autonomous goal completion (like AutoGPT (Richards, 2023), BabyAGI (Nakajima, 2023), AgentGPT (age, 2023)) as well as LLM agents situated in social and game contexts (Park et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2023b; Zhu et al., 2023), sparking substantial public interest and discussions.
items.zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）（Brown et al., 2020; Chowdhery et al., 2022; Touvron et al., 2023）的出现，例如GPT-4（OpenAI, 2023），为这一领域带来了许多新机遇。通过广泛的对齐训练（Ouyang et al., 2022; Wei et al., 2022a; Sanh et al., 2022），LLM不仅掌握了传统的NLP任务，还展现出理解人类意图和执行指令的惊人能力。这推动了各种基于LLM的自主目标完成应用（如AutoGPT（Richards, 2023）、BabyAGI（Nakajima, 2023）、AgentGPT（age, 2023））以及社交和游戏场景中的LLM智能体（Park et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2023b; Zhu et al., 2023）的发展，引发了公众的广泛兴趣和讨论。

items.en: Despite these advancements, the lack of a systematic and standard benchmark to evaluate LLM-as-Agent presents a critical challenge. Historically, text-based game environments (Osborne et al., 2022; Côté et al., 2019; Hausknecht et al., 2020; Urbanek et al., 2019) have been employed for language agent evaluation. But they often suffer from the limitation of closed, discrete action spaces, as well as their primarily narrow focus on models’ commonsense grounding. More recently, attempts on embodied agents (Reed et al., 2022; Huang et al., 2022; Ahn et al., 2022) have employed complicated multi-modal simulators based on games (Küttler et al., 2020; Fan et al., 2022), GUI (Shi et al., 2017; Toyama et al., 2021), and indoor scenes (Shen et al., 2021; Srivastava et al., 2022). However, these simulators, despite their complexity, do not accurately reflect the practical use cases of LLMs, and their multi-modal nature creates a hurdle for the urgent evaluation of existing text-only LLMs. Finally, most benchmarks now for agents focus on single environments and thus fail to provide a comprehensive overview of LLMs across diverse application scenarios.
items.zh: 尽管取得了这些进展，但缺乏系统化、标准化的基准来评估LLM作为智能体，这构成了一个关键挑战。历史上，基于文本的游戏环境（Osborne et al., 2022; Côté et al., 2019; Hausknecht et al., 2020; Urbanek et al., 2019）被用于语言智能体评估。但它们通常受限于封闭、离散的动作空间，以及主要关注模型的常识基础。最近，关于具身智能体的尝试（Reed et al., 2022; Huang et al., 2022; Ahn et al., 2022）采用了基于游戏（Küttler et al., 2020; Fan et al., 2022）、GUI（Shi et al., 2017; Toyama et al., 2021）和室内场景（Shen et al., 2021; Srivastava et al., 2022）的复杂多模态模拟器。然而，这些模拟器尽管复杂，并不能准确反映LLM的实际使用场景，其多模态特性也为当前仅文本LLM的迫切评估设置了障碍。最后，目前大多数针对智能体的基准测试只关注单一环境，因此无法提供LLM在不同应用场景中的全面概览。

items.capEn: Figure 2: AgentBench is the first systematic benchmark to evaluate LLM-as-Agent on a wide array of real-world challenges and 8 distinct environments. In total, 27 LLMs are examined in this edition.
items.capZh: 图2：AgentBench是首个系统化基准测试，用于评估LLM作为智能体在广泛现实挑战和8个不同环境中的表现。本版本共检验了27个LLM。

items.en: To address these challenges, we introduce AgentBench, a multi-dimensional benchmark designed to evaluate LLM-as-Agent across a spectrum of different environments. AgentBench encompasses eight distinct environments (Cf. Figure 4), which could be categorized into three types of groundings:
items.zh: 为应对这些挑战，我们引入了AgentBench，一个多维度基准测试，旨在评估LLM作为智能体在多种不同环境中的表现。AgentBench包含八个不同的环境（参见图4），可分为三类基础：

items.en: Code: Operating System, Database, Knowledge Graph (Anonymous, 2023)
items.zh: 代码：操作系统、数据库、知识图谱（Anonymous, 2023）

items.en: Game: Digital Card Game, Lateral Thinking Puzzles, House-Holding (Shridhar et al., 2020b)
items.zh: 游戏：数字卡牌游戏、横向思维谜题、家务（Shridhar et al., 2020b）

items.en: Web: Web Shopping (Yao et al., 2022), Web Browsing (Deng et al., 2023)
items.zh: 网络：网络购物（Yao et al., 2022）、网络浏览（Deng et al., 2023）

items.en: All datasets, whether newly created or adapted from existent ones, are meticulously designed and reformulated to simulate interactive environments where text-only LLMs can operate as autonomous agents. AgentBench thus systematically evaluate an LLM’s core abilities, including following instructions (Ouyang et al., 2022), coding (Chen et al., 2021), knowledge acquisition (Joshi et al., 2017; Talmor et al., 2019), logical reasoning (Srivastava et al., 2023), and commonsense grounding (Shridhar et al., 2020a). It serves as an ideal testbed for both LLM and agent evaluation.
items.zh: 所有数据集，无论是新创建的还是从现有数据集改编的，都经过精心设计和重构，以模拟仅文本LLM可作为自主智能体运行的交互式环境。因此，AgentBench系统性地评估了LLM的核心能力，包括指令遵循（Ouyang et al., 2022）、编码（Chen et al., 2021）、知识获取（Joshi et al., 2017; Talmor et al., 2019）、逻辑推理（Srivastava et al., 2023）和常识基础（Shridhar et al., 2020a）。它既是LLM评估也是智能体评估的理想测试平台。

items.en: In addition, we develop a unified evaluation toolkit for LLMs to operate on diverse customized agent tasks, thus enabling a comprehensive benchmarking of the LLM-as-Agent ability of 27 different LLMs on AgentBench, including both API-based and OSS models. Our results reveal that top-tier models like GPT-4 are capable of handling a wide array of real-world tasks, indicating the potential for developing a potent, continuously learning agent. However, we also note a significant performance gap between these top-tier models and their OSS competitors. Despite the recent success of OSS LLMs and their competitive scores on several benchmarks (Li et al., 2023; Chen et al., 2021; Cobbe et al., 2021), their performance on the challenging AgentBench tasks lags considerably. This underscores the necessity for additional efforts to enhance the learning abilities of OSS LLMs.
items.zh: 此外，我们开发了一个统一的评估工具包，使LLM能够在各种定制的智能体任务上运行，从而在AgentBench上对27个不同LLM（包括基于API和开源模型）的LLM作为智能体能力进行全面基准测试。我们的结果显示，像GPT-4这样的顶级模型能够处理广泛的现实世界任务，表明开发一个强大的、持续学习的智能体具有潜力。然而，我们也注意到这些顶级模型与其开源竞争对手之间存在显著的性能差距。尽管开源LLM最近取得了成功，并在多个基准测试中获得了有竞争力的分数（Li et al., 2023; Chen et al., 2021; Cobbe et al., 2021），但它们在具有挑战性的AgentBench任务上的表现却远远落后。这凸显了需要额外努力来增强开源LLM的学习能力。

items.en: We identify portions of agent task failures in different environments and LLMs, unveiling the insufficient abilities of long-term reasoning, decision-making, and instruction following in existing LLMs. Comparisons between different LLMs manifest that a proper strategy of introducing code training can help improve LLM-as-Agent. Alignment training over high-quality data (e.g., data generated by gpt-4) could also help improve LLM agents. In summary, our contributions are:
items.zh: 我们识别了不同环境和LLM中智能体任务失败的部分原因，揭示了现有LLM在长期推理、决策和指令遵循能力方面的不足。不同LLM之间的比较表明，引入代码训练的适当策略有助于提升LLM作为智能体的能力。在高质量数据（例如由gpt-4生成的数据）上进行对齐训练也有助于改进LLM智能体。总之，我们的贡献如下：

items.en: We introduce the concept of evaluating LLMs as agents and present AgentBench, a comprehensive benchmark to standardize the evaluation. It defines eight distinct environments of 3 types based on real-world scenarios, offering a practical testbed for LLMs’ wide array of capabilities.
items.zh: 我们引入了评估LLM作为智能体的概念，并提出了AgentBench，一个全面的基准测试以标准化评估。它定义了基于现实世界场景的3种类型的8个不同环境，为LLM的广泛能力提供了实用的测试平台。

items.en: We perform a thorough evaluation of 27 different LLMs using AgentBench, uncovering a significant performance gap between leading API-based commercial LLMs and OSS models. We also quantitatively analyze the reasons for failures in existing LLM agents and highlight directions for improvement, such as code training and higher-quality alignment data.
items.zh: 我们使用AgentBench对27个不同的LLM进行了彻底评估，揭示了领先的基于API的商业LLM与开源模型之间的显著性能差距。我们还定量分析了现有LLM智能体失败的原因，并强调了改进方向，例如代码训练和更高质量的对齐数据。

items.en: To facilitate the evaluation of LLM-as-Agent, we have introduced an integrated toolkit grounded in the Server-Client architecture, focusing on modular and scalable design principles. This enables easy customization of model assessments for any LLMs using the HTTP protocol. Complemented by its associated datasets and environments, this toolkit is now openly accessible to the broader research community.
items.zh: 为促进LLM作为智能体的评估，我们引入了一个基于服务器-客户端架构的集成工具包，专注于模块化和可扩展的设计原则。这使得任何LLM都可以通过HTTP协议轻松定制模型评估。结合其相关数据集和环境，该工具包现已向更广泛的研究社区开放。

full.secZh: 2 大语言模型作为智能体：定义与基础

items.en: Here, we formalize the terms for describing the evaluation of LLMs as agents and the necessary preliminary knowledge for using LLMs in the context of agent evaluation.
items.zh: 在此，我们正式定义描述LLM作为智能体评估的术语，以及在智能体评估背景下使用LLM所需的初步知识。

items.en: Definition: Interactive Evaluation of LLM-as-Agent. The interactive evaluation of LLM-as-Agent could be regarded as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (𝒮,𝒜,𝒯,ℛ,𝒰,𝒪𝒮𝒜𝒯ℛ𝒰𝒪\mathcal{S},\mathcal{A},\mathcal{T},\mathcal{R},\mathcal{U},\mathcal{O}), which comprises state space 𝒮𝒮\mathcal{S}, action space 𝒜𝒜\mathcal{A}, transition function 𝒯:𝒮×𝒜→𝒮:𝒯→𝒮𝒜𝒮\mathcal{T}:\mathcal{S}\times\mathcal{A}\to\mathcal{S}, reward assigning function ℛℛ\mathcal{R}, task instruction space 𝒰𝒰\mathcal{U}, and observation space 𝒪𝒪\mathcal{O}. Here, we denote an LLM agent as ℳℳ\mathcal{M}.
items.zh: 定义：LLM作为智能体的交互式评估。LLM作为智能体的交互式评估可视为一个部分可观察马尔可夫决策过程 (𝒮,𝒜,𝒯,ℛ,𝒰,𝒪)，包括状态空间𝒮、动作空间𝒜、转移函数𝒯: 𝒮×𝒜→𝒮、奖励分配函数ℛ、任务指令空间𝒰和观察空间𝒪。这里，我们将LLM智能体记为ℳ。

items.en: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) and Other Reasoning Strategies. Since LLM-as-Agent requires LLMs’ strong reasoning ability, CoT (Wei et al., 2022b), which has been considered a de facto strategy in related evaluation together with actions (Yao et al., 2023b), is also adopted in AgentBench. Despite many improved strategies proposed later, such as introducing ensemble (Wang et al., 2023c), reflection (Shinn et al., 2023), and search (Yao et al., 2023a), we evaluate LLMs with the most primitive CoT in AgentBench. Without multiple trials, repeated generations, or complicated strategies, CoT is the easiest, cheapest, and most common way for people to deploy LLM agents.
items.zh: 思维链（CoT）及其他推理策略。由于LLM作为智能体需要LLM具备强大的推理能力，CoT（Wei et al., 2022b）在相关评估中与动作一起被视为事实上的策略（Yao et al., 2023b），并在AgentBench中也被采用。尽管后来提出了许多改进策略，如引入集成（Wang et al., 2023c）、反思（Shinn et al., 2023）和搜索（Yao et al., 2023a），我们在AgentBench中使用最原始的CoT评估LLM。无需多次试验、重复生成或复杂策略，CoT是人们部署LLM智能体最简单、最便宜且最常见的方式。

items.en: Typical Types of Finish Reasons. Despite LLMs’ capabilities, we show in AgentBench that even the strongest gpt-4 is not qualified as a practically usable agent. We identify and categorize finish reasons of LLM agents on AgentBench tasks into five typical types:
items.zh: 典型的结束原因类型。尽管LLM具备能力，我们在AgentBench中表明，即使是最强的gpt-4也不足以成为实际可用的智能体。我们将AgentBench任务中LLM智能体的结束原因识别并分类为五种典型类型：

items.en: Context Limit Exceeded (CLE): the length of interaction history exceeds the LLM’s maximum context length (only happened in 2,048-length LLMs text-davinci-002 and 003).
items.zh: 上下文长度超限（CLE）：交互历史长度超过LLM的最大上下文长度（仅发生在2,048长度的LLM text-davinci-002和003中）。

items.en: Invalid Format (IF): the agent does not follow the format instruction.
items.zh: 格式无效（IF）：智能体未遵循格式指令。

items.en: Invalid Action (IA): the agent follows the format instruction, but its selected action is invalid.
items.zh: 动作无效（IA）：智能体遵循了格式指令，但所选动作无效。

items.en: Task Limit Exceeded (TLE): the agent does not solve the problem after reaching the predefined maximum interaction turns or begins to do repeated generations for many turns.
items.zh: 任务限制超限（TLE）：智能体在达到预定义的最大交互轮次后仍未解决问题，或开始重复生成多轮。

items.en: and Complete (task ends normally). While IF and IA are mostly caused by LLMs’ poor instruction following, TLE often indicates a weak multi-turn ability in certain tasks.
items.zh: 以及完成（任务正常结束）。虽然IF和IA主要由LLM的指令遵循能力差引起，但TLE通常表明在某些任务中多轮能力较弱。

full.sec: 3 Composition of AgentBench: A Brief Look

items.en: In this section, we briefly introduce the datasets and environments that compose the AgentBench. Compared to previous agent evaluation benchmarks (Côté et al., 2019; Fan et al., 2022), AgentBench concentrates on the practical evaluation of LLMs via Chain-of-Thought (CoT) (Wei et al., 2022b; Yao et al., 2023b) prompting, including code-grounded, game-grounded, and web-grounded scenarios. They pinpoint promising directions of LLMs’ applications with autonomous mission completion, and their versatility avoids task-specific models’ (e.g., code-specific LLMs) overperformance on AgentBench. Due to page limit, for details of construction, evaluation, and prompt examples, please refer to Appendix.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们简要介绍构成AgentBench的数据集和环境。与之前的智能体评估基准（Côté等人，2019；Fan等人，2022）相比，AgentBench通过思维链（CoT）（Wei等人，2022b；Yao等人，2023b）提示专注于LLM的实际评估，包括代码基础、游戏基础和网页基础场景。它们指出了LLM在自主完成任务方面的有前景的应用方向，其多样性避免了特定任务模型（例如，代码专用LLM）在AgentBench上的过度表现。由于篇幅限制，有关构建、评估和提示示例的详细信息，请参阅附录。

items.en: Since LLMs can generate high quality codes (Chen et al., 2021), a very practical mission for LLM agents is to assist human interaction with computer interfaces. Here, we introduce three three environments depending on coding and reasoning abilities as representatives in AgentBench.
items.zh: 由于LLM可以生成高质量代码（Chen等人，2021），LLM智能体一个非常实际的任务是协助人类与计算机界面交互。在这里，我们介绍三个依赖于编码和推理能力的环境，作为AgentBench中的代表。

items.en: Operating System (OS). Allowing LLMs to access and manipulate OS in the terminal is a fascinating but challenging mission. Despite attempts on translating natural language to Shell commands (Lin et al., 2018), few prior efforts evaluate models in executable environments. We aim to evaluate LLMs in genuine OS’ interactive bash environments (i.e., Ubuntu Docker (Merkel et al., 2014)) on human questions with deterministic answers (e.g., number of users with non-/home directories in an OS.) or series of operations for practical goals (e.g., recursively set all directory files to read-only, excluding mine). We adopt the success rate (SR) as the evaluation metric. (Cf. Appendix B for more details)
items.zh: 操作系统（OS）。允许LLM在终端中访问和操作操作系统是一个迷人但具有挑战性的任务。尽管有将自然语言翻译为Shell命令的尝试（Lin等人，2018），但很少有先前的工作在可执行环境中评估模型。我们旨在评估LLM在真实操作系统的交互式bash环境（即Ubuntu Docker（Merkel等人，2014））中，针对具有确定性答案的人类问题（例如，操作系统中具有非/home目录的用户数量）或为实现实际目标的一系列操作（例如，递归设置所有目录文件为只读，排除我的文件）。我们采用成功率（SR）作为评估指标。（更多细节参见附录B）

items.en: Database (DB). As database analysis is crucial but also difficult in many daily affairs, it is paramount to examine LLMs’ abilities to operate on real databases via SQL. Prior research has a significant emphasis on individual procedures, such as translation between SQL and natural language (Zhong et al., 2017), or answering questions given individual small tables (Nan et al., 2021; Iyyer et al., 2017). However, few consider evaluating models on the complete pipeline as a whole. Therefore, AgentBench evaluates LLMs on authentic SQL interfaces, databases, multiple tables, and different types of queries as is in the real world. We adopt the SR as the main evaluation metric. (Cf. Appendix C for more details)
items.zh: 数据库（DB）。由于数据库分析在日常事务中至关重要但也困难，检查LLM通过SQL操作真实数据库的能力至关重要。先前的研究显著侧重于单个过程，例如SQL与自然语言之间的翻译（Zhong等人，2017），或针对单个小表格回答问题（Nan等人，2021；Iyyer等人，2017）。然而，很少有人考虑将模型作为整体在完整流水线上进行评估。因此，AgentBench在真实的SQL接口、数据库、多个表格和不同类型的查询上评估LLM，如同现实世界一样。我们采用SR作为主要评估指标。（更多细节参见附录C）

items.en: Knowledge Graph (KG (Anonymous, 2023)). Engaging with contemporary KGs, which are often vast in size (e.g., Freebase (Bollacker et al., 2008) has over 45M entities and 3B facts), demands a broad range of skills from an intelligent agent (Gu et al., 2023). Operating in such environments, which are only partially observable, requires the agent to make decisions with incomplete information and manage inherent uncertainties with various skills, including language understanding (e.g., intricacies and subtleties), planning (e.g., breaking down instructions into more manageable components), and tool using (e.g., interact with KG interfaces). As a result, we propose KG as a representative testing ground to assess the decision-making abilities of AI agents. We adopt question answering as the basic task formulation and consequently the answer F1 as the metric. (Cf. Appendix D for more details)
items.zh: 知识图谱（KG（Anonymous，2023））。与当代知识图谱交互，这些知识图谱通常规模庞大（例如，Freebase（Bollacker等人，2008）拥有超过4500万个实体和30亿个事实），需要智能体具备广泛的技能（Gu等人，2023）。在这种仅部分可观察的环境中操作，要求智能体在不完整信息下做出决策，并利用各种技能管理固有的不确定性，包括语言理解（例如，复杂性和细微差别）、规划（例如，将指令分解为更易管理的部分）和工具使用（例如，与KG界面交互）。因此，我们提出KG作为评估AI智能体决策能力的代表性测试场。我们采用问答作为基本任务形式，并因此采用答案F1作为指标。（更多细节参见附录D）

items.en: Playing games usually requires strong capabilities in designing strategies, following instructions, and reasoning. Compared to code-grounded, tasks in game-grounded environments require no expertise in coding but more integral grasping of commonsense and world knowledge.
items.zh: 玩游戏通常需要强大的策略设计、指令遵循和推理能力。与代码基础环境相比，游戏基础环境中的任务不需要编码专业知识，但需要更全面地掌握常识和世界知识。

items.en: Digital Card Game (DCG). Games, especially those that require strategies and planning, could serve as simulated environments for intelligent agent development. DCG (e.g., Hearthstone (Hoover et al., 2020)), instead, is an ideal option for text-only LLM evaluation. It usually involves abundant text descriptions for cards, turn-based competition, and thoughtful playing strategies to win, testing a model’s understanding of game rules, operating logic, and abilities to form strategic decisions based on current conditions and past experiences in the game.
items.zh: 数字卡牌游戏（DCG）。游戏，尤其是那些需要策略和规划的游戏，可以作为智能体开发的模拟环境。DCG（例如，炉石传说（Hoover等人，2020））是纯文本LLM评估的理想选择。它通常涉及丰富的卡牌文本描述、回合制竞争和深思熟虑的获胜策略，测试模型对游戏规则、操作逻辑的理解，以及基于当前条件和游戏过去经验形成战略决策的能力。

items.en: In AgentBench we adapt a simplified DCG system—Aquawar111https://www.saiblo.net/—from the 2021 Tsinghua University Agent Competition (THUAC) hosted by Student Association for Science and Technology in Department of Computer Science and Technology (CST-SAST), for evaluating LLM-as-Agent. In Aquawar, the agent acts as a player managing a team of fishes with different talents to battle against another team (controlled by our ad-hoc baseline agent) in a turn-based form. We report LLMs’ win rate as the evaluation metric. (Cf. Appendix E for more details)
items.zh: 在AgentBench中，我们改编了一个简化的DCG系统——Aquawar111https://www.saiblo.net/——来自2021年清华大学智能体竞赛（THUAC），由计算机科学与技术系学生科协（CST-SAST）主办，用于评估LLM作为智能体。在Aquawar中，智能体作为玩家管理一支由不同天赋的鱼组成的队伍，以回合制形式与另一支队伍（由我们的临时基线智能体控制）对战。我们报告LLM的胜率作为评估指标。（更多细节参见附录E）

items.en: Lateral Thinking Puzzles (LTP). Lateral thinking puzzles (Sloane, 1992), or situation puzzles, 海龟汤, is a popular group-playing game around the world. The game usually has a person hosting the puzzle and others guess by asking riddle-related questions. The host can only respond “yes”, “no”, or “irrelevant”. The game is terminated when one of the player recovers the critical plots of the puzzle. Its name derives from the psychological term “lateral thinking” (De Bono, 1970), which refers to the ability of deducing facts from unconventional perspectives and exploring new ideas.
items.zh: 横向思维谜题（LTP）。横向思维谜题（Sloane，1992），或情境谜题，海龟汤，是一种全球流行的群体游戏。游戏通常由一个人主持谜题，其他人通过询问与谜语相关的问题来猜测。主持人只能回答“是”、“否”或“无关”。当其中一名玩家恢复谜题的关键情节时，游戏结束。其名称源于心理学术语“横向思维”（De Bono，1970），指从非常规角度推断事实和探索新想法的能力。

items.en: In this dataset, we first set up an LTP host system for automatic judging (Cf. Appendix F). To assess LLMs’ lateral reasoning prowess, a diverse puzzle dataset is curated from web of varied levels of difficulty. We break down the true plot into several bullets and measure the portion of guessed-out bullets (i.e., game progress) when an agent exhausted the maximum number of playing rounds as the evaluation metric. Through this assessment, we aim to gain insights into the depth and agility of LLMs’ lateral reasoning abilities. (Cf. Appendix F for more details)
items.zh: 在该数据集中，我们首先建立了一个LTP主持系统用于自动评判（参见附录F）。为了评估LLM的横向推理能力，我们从网络上整理了一个包含不同难度级别的多样化谜题数据集。我们将真实情节分解为多个要点，并测量当智能体用尽最大游戏轮数时猜出的要点比例（即游戏进度）作为评估指标。通过这一评估，我们旨在深入了解LLM横向推理能力的深度和敏捷性。（更多细节参见附录F）

items.en: House-Holding (HH, ALFWorld (Shridhar et al., 2020b)). Embodied game environments such as house-holding, which require strong commonsense grounding, have been well-established for language agent evaluation (Côté et al., 2019). In AgentBench, we assess the model’s capability in accomplishing tasks in physical house-holding environments on the classical ALFWorld (Shridhar et al., 2020b) derived from the well-established text-game toolkit TextWorld (Côté et al., 2019). The agent needs to accomplish house-holding tasks such as “Put a pan on the dining table”. We adopt the SR as the evaluation metric. (Cf. Appendix G for more details)
items.zh: 家务（HH，ALFWorld（Shridhar等人，2020b））。具身游戏环境，如家务，需要强大的常识基础，已被广泛用于语言智能体评估（Côté等人，2019）。在AgentBench中，我们在经典的ALFWorld（Shridhar等人，2020b）上评估模型在物理家务环境中完成任务的能力，该环境源自成熟的文本游戏工具包TextWorld（Côté等人，2019）。智能体需要完成家务任务，例如“将平底锅放在餐桌上”。我们采用SR作为评估指标。（更多细节参见附录G）

items.en: Web pages have been primary interfaces for people to interact in the real world. Thus, assessing LLM agents’ behaviors in complex web environments would be critical and valuable for following development. Here, we adapt two existing web browsing datasets for practical evaluation over LLMs.
items.zh: 网页一直是人们在现实世界中交互的主要界面。因此，评估LLM智能体在复杂网页环境中的行为对于后续发展至关重要且具有价值。在这里，我们改编了两个现有的网页浏览数据集，用于对LLM进行实际评估。

items.en: Web Shopping (WS, WebShop (Yao et al., 2022)). Online shopping is a very practical and important part of modern life. Its trajectory, which comprises searching, viewing, and choosing desirable items on a real e-commerce website, requires autonomous agents’ strong reasoning and decision-making abilities. Webshop (Yao et al., 2022), a simulated online shopping environment, exactly serves such a purpose for evaluating language agents. While it is originally evaluated on specifically trained models, we propose assessing LLMs with mere prompting. (Cf. Appendix H for more details)
items.zh: 网络购物（WS，WebShop（Yao等人，2022））。在线购物是现代生活中非常实用且重要的部分。其轨迹包括在真实电子商务网站上搜索、查看和选择所需物品，需要自主智能体具备强大的推理和决策能力。Webshop（Yao等人，2022）是一个模拟的在线购物环境，正好用于评估语言智能体。虽然它最初是在专门训练的模型上评估的，但我们提出仅通过提示来评估LLM。（更多细节参见附录H）

items.en: Web Browsing (WB, Mind2Web (Deng et al., 2023)). General web environment is an ideal sandbox for training and evaluating intelligent agents. Mind2Web (Deng et al., 2023) is a very recently released general benchmark for developing and assessing web agents capable of executing intricate tasks across various website domains, given high-level user instructions. It designs feasible actions for website interactions, such as clicking, selecting, and typing, thereby facilitating a holistic evaluation of LLMs as web agents. Compared to Mind2Web’s original setting, we make adaptations to allow its evaluation on prompted LLMs without additional fine-tuning. (Cf. Appendix I for more details)
items.zh: 网页浏览（WB，Mind2Web（Deng等人，2023））。通用网页环境是训练和评估智能体的理想沙盒。Mind2Web（Deng等人，2023）是一个最近发布的通用基准，用于开发和评估能够根据高级用户指令在各种网站领域执行复杂任务的网页智能体。它设计了可行的网站交互动作，如点击、选择和输入，从而促进对LLM作为网页智能体的全面评估。与Mind2Web的原始设置相比，我们进行了调整，以允许其在无需额外微调的情况下对提示LLM进行评估。（更多细节参见附录I）

items.en: We extensively evaluate 27 LLMs, including API-based commercial models and open-sourced LLMs, to form a systematic view of the existing performance of LLM-as-Agent. We also design and release a simple plug-and-play evaluation toolkit to facilitate related LLM-as-Agent research.
items.zh: 我们广泛评估了27个LLM，包括基于API的商业模型和开源LLM，以形成对LLM作为智能体现有性能的系统性视图。我们还设计并发布了一个简单的即插即用评估工具包，以促进相关的LLM作为智能体研究。

items.en: Dataset Statistics. We report the statistics of datasets in AgentBench in Table 2. For simplicity, we use the abbreviation of each dataset in the following part. All datasets are practical multi-turn interacting challenges, and their estimated solving turns for each individual problem range from 5 to 50. We provide two splits for each dataset: Dev and Test. The Dev split’s all environments, answers, and checking scripts are public, while the Test is kept.
items.zh: 数据集统计。我们在表2中报告了AgentBench的数据集统计。为简洁起见，下文使用每个数据集的缩写。所有数据集都是实际的多轮交互挑战，每个问题的估计解决轮次在5到50之间。我们为每个数据集提供两个划分：开发集和测试集。开发集的所有环境、答案和检查脚本都是公开的，而测试集则保密。

items.en: We also carefully balance the evaluation comprehensiveness and efficiency in AgentBench design, as LLMs’ multi-turn interaction can be time-consuming. We set the size of Dev and Test to 269 and 1,091, respectively, resulting in around 4k and 13k calls for inference, approximately the identical amounts of calls for inference as MMLU (Hendrycks et al., 2021b) requires.
items.zh: 我们还在AgentBench设计中仔细平衡了评估的全面性和效率，因为LLM的多轮交互可能耗时。我们将开发集和测试集的大小分别设为269和1,091，导致大约4k和13k次推理调用，与MMLU（Hendrycks等人，2021b）所需的推理调用数量大致相同。

items.en: LLMs to Evaluate. As a systematic attempt to benchmark existing LLMs on LLM-as-Agent, we include in total 27 models for evaluation, which could be roughly classified into two categories:
items.zh: 待评估的LLM。作为对现有LLM在LLM-as-Agent方面进行基准测试的系统性尝试，我们总共纳入了27个模型进行评估，这些模型大致可分为两类：

items.en: API-based Commercial LLMs: mainly consist of LLM APIs without disclosed parameter amounts (Cf. Table 1). Due to more investments, their performances are usually better.
items.zh: 基于API的商业LLM：主要包括未公开参数量的LLM API（见表1）。由于投入更多，它们的性能通常更好。

items.en: Open-sourced (OSS) LLMs: mostly come from the academia and some companies (Cf. Table 1). Due to limited computing resources, we only include OSS LLMs smaller than 70B here.
items.zh: 开源（OSS）LLM：主要来自学术界和一些公司（见表1）。由于计算资源有限，我们这里只包含小于70B的开源LLM。

items.en: Toolkit: Streamlining LLM Evaluation with API-Centric Approach and Environment Isolation. As Language Model (LLM) systems continue to advance in complexity and are primarily accessible through APIs, we have developed an evaluation toolkit that aligns with the API-oriented philosophy. This toolkit is meticulously designed to interact with APIs, simplifying the process of adapting and testing different LLMs. Researchers interested in evaluating their LLMs on AgentBench only need to set up a model server accessible via the HTTP protocol.
items.zh: 工具包：通过以API为中心的方法和环境隔离简化LLM评估。随着语言模型（LLM）系统在复杂性上不断进步且主要通过API访问，我们开发了一个与API导向理念一致的评估工具包。该工具包精心设计用于与API交互，简化了适配和测试不同LLM的过程。有兴趣在AgentBench上评估其LLM的研究人员只需设置一个可通过HTTP协议访问的模型服务器。

items.en: Moreover, dealing with diverse and intricate interaction environments poses a significant challenge. Uniformly configuring all these environments can be arduous and may lead to conflicts. To address this, we have implemented two key strategies. Firstly, we encapsulate tasks with complex environments into Docker images. Researchers can effortlessly utilize these images by mounting the code path and initiating the evaluation process with ease. Secondly, we have subdivided each task into separate workers, ensuring that the environments of these tasks remain isolated and free from conflicts. (Refer to Appendix A for further details.)
items.zh: 此外，处理多样且复杂的交互环境是一个重大挑战。统一配置所有这些环境可能很繁琐，并可能导致冲突。为了解决这个问题，我们实施了两个关键策略。首先，我们将具有复杂环境的任务封装到Docker镜像中。研究人员可以通过挂载代码路径并轻松启动评估过程来使用这些镜像。其次，我们将每个任务细分为独立的工作单元，确保这些任务的环境保持隔离且无冲突。（更多细节请参见附录A。）

items.en: Evaluation Prompt Setup. To accommodate the majority of existing dialogue models, our dialogue paradigm is structured around two roles, user (i.e., instruction & environment feedback) and agent, engaging and alternating with one another. We record interaction trajectories as a conversation history (u0,a0,⋯,uk,ak)subscript𝑢0subscript𝑎0⋯subscript𝑢𝑘subscript𝑎𝑘(u_{0},a_{0},\cdots,u_{k},a_{k}) involving the user and agent, where uisubscript𝑢𝑖u_{i}, aisubscript𝑎𝑖a_{i} represents the i𝑖i-th round of the conversation history. When we perform inference, the conversation history must be like (u0,a0,⋯,uk)subscript𝑢0subscript𝑎0⋯subscript𝑢𝑘(u_{0},a_{0},\cdots,u_{k}). We select the minimum r𝑟r such that count of all tokens222Because the tokenizers of each model is different, we simply calculate tokens like this: a word with length n𝑛n occupies ⌈n/6⌉𝑛6\lceil n/6\rceil token(s), and a non-blank character takes 111 token. in (u0,ar,ur+1,⋯,uk)subscript𝑢0subscript𝑎𝑟subscript𝑢𝑟1⋯subscript𝑢𝑘(u_{0},a_{r},u_{r+1},\cdots,u_{k}) is not greater than 3500. And then we append "[NOTICE] 2​r2𝑟2r messages are omitted." into u0subscript𝑢0u_{0}. After that, the sequence (u0,ar,ur+1,⋯,uk)subscript𝑢0subscript𝑎𝑟subscript𝑢𝑟1⋯subscript𝑢𝑘(u_{0},a_{r},u_{r+1},\cdots,u_{k}) is regarded as the final input in multi-turn chat format.
items.zh: 评估提示设置。为了适应大多数现有的对话模型，我们的对话范式围绕两个角色构建：用户（即指令和环境反馈）和智能体，两者交替进行。我们将交互轨迹记录为涉及用户和智能体的对话历史 (u0,a0,⋯,uk,ak)，其中 ui, ai 表示对话历史的第 i 轮。当我们进行推理时，对话历史必须形如 (u0,a0,⋯,uk)。我们选择最小的 r，使得 (u0,ar,ur+1,⋯,uk) 中所有token的数量不超过3500。然后我们将 "[NOTICE] 2r 条消息被省略。" 附加到 u0 中。之后，序列 (u0,ar,ur+1,⋯,uk) 被视为多轮聊天格式的最终输入。

items.en: However, in order to consider non-chat models, we append a post-processor. We feed the history into the model for chat models supporting multiple turns. For models supporting only text completion (e.g., text-davinci-003), we prepend "USER:" or "AGENT:" into each item in the history and finally append the string "AGENT:" to make models generate the agent’s content.
items.zh: 然而，为了考虑非聊天模型，我们附加了一个后处理器。对于支持多轮聊天的聊天模型，我们将历史输入模型。对于仅支持文本补全的模型（例如 text-davinci-003），我们在历史中的每个项目前添加 "USER:" 或 "AGENT:"，最后附加字符串 "AGENT:" 以使模型生成智能体的内容。

items.en: For task prompt organization, we adapted the format from (Yao et al., 2023b) to include both “Thought” (for CoT) and “Action” but in one single turn. Usually, a simple CoT demonstration is provided in the task instruction for a better output format. To ensure reproducible results, we set temperature=0 (i.e., greedy decoding) in the inference on all tasks following (Wei et al., 2022b).
items.zh: 对于任务提示的组织，我们改编了（Yao等人，2023b）的格式，在一轮中同时包含“思考”（用于思维链）和“行动”。通常，在任务指令中会提供一个简单的思维链示例以获得更好的输出格式。为确保结果可重复，我们在所有任务的推理中设置 temperature=0（即贪婪解码），遵循（Wei等人，2022b）。

items.en: Overall Score Calculation. We have observed that the score distribution for each task varies significantly as tasks differ in difficulty levels. As a consequence, a naively averaged score is heavily impacted by tasks that generally yield higher scores (e.g., Web Shopping in our observation), overshadowing those with lower scores and being unsuitable for AgentBench’s purpose.
items.zh: 总体得分计算。我们观察到每个任务的得分分布因任务难度不同而有显著差异。因此，简单平均的得分会受到通常得分较高任务（例如我们观察中的Web Shopping）的严重影响，掩盖了得分较低的任务，不适合AgentBench的目的。

items.en: Therefore, we produce the overall score by first resizing each task’s average score to 111 across all the models we evaluate and then averaging the scores across all tasks for each model (Cf. Table 2). To standardize and simplify score calculations for future studies, we utilize the reciprocal average score of all the tested LLMs in each task as a fixed weight for future overall score calculation. The total score is then computed as the average value obtained by multiplying the score of each task by its corresponding weight. This method ensures fairness and consistency in evaluation, enabling easier comparisons and analysis in future research.
items.zh: 因此，我们首先生成总体得分，方法是将每个任务的平均得分在我们评估的所有模型上缩放到1，然后对每个模型在所有任务上的得分进行平均（见表2）。为了标准化和简化未来研究的得分计算，我们使用每个任务中所有测试LLM的倒数平均得分作为未来总体得分计算的固定权重。总得分则通过将每个任务的得分乘以其对应权重后取平均值来计算。这种方法确保了评估的公平性和一致性，便于未来研究中的比较和分析。

items.en: Overall and dataset-specific scores in AgentBench are reported in Table 3. Surprisingly, on this challenging benchmark, we discover that some top LLMs are equipped with solid capabilities for dealing with real-world environmental interaction. For example, gpt-4 presents the best performance on 6 out of 8 datasets in AgentBench; on HH, it achieves a success rate of 78%, indicating its practical usability in this scenario. claude-2 and claude follow gpt-4 but quite outperform gpt-3.5-turbo. Despite other API-based LLMs’ relatively poorer performance, regardless of tasks, most of them can solve quite a few percent of problems. All API-based LLMs have an AgentBench overall score above 1.00.
items.zh: AgentBench中的总体和数据集特定得分报告在表3中。令人惊讶的是，在这个具有挑战性的基准测试中，我们发现一些顶级LLM具备处理现实环境交互的扎实能力。例如，gpt-4在AgentBench的8个数据集中有6个表现最佳；在HH上，它达到了78%的成功率，表明其在该场景下的实际可用性。claude-2和claude紧随gpt-4之后，但明显优于gpt-3.5-turbo。尽管其他基于API的LLM性能相对较差，但无论任务如何，它们大多能解决一定比例的问题。所有基于API的LLM的AgentBench总体得分均高于1.00。

items.en: OSS LLMs, however, commonly fail to solve problems in some challenging tasks, such as KG, DCG, and HH. We plot their performance concerning their sizes in Figure 3. Generally, most open-sourced LLMs perform far poorer than API-based LLMs in AgentBench (Avg. 0.51 v.s. 2.15). The most capable OSS LLM turns out to be codellama-34b, achieving an overall score of 0.96 but still presents a clear performance gap to gpt-3.5-turbo. This contrasts recent claims that some OSS LLMs are comparable to gpt-3.5-turbo and gpt-4. We still need much effort to produce stronger OSS LLMs to serve agent purposes.
items.zh: 然而，开源LLM通常无法解决一些具有挑战性任务中的问题，例如KG、DCG和HH。我们在图3中绘制了它们性能与模型大小的关系。总体而言，大多数开源LLM在AgentBench上的表现远差于基于API的LLM（平均0.51对2.15）。能力最强的开源LLM是codellama-34b，总体得分为0.96，但与gpt-3.5-turbo相比仍有明显差距。这与最近一些声称某些开源LLM可与gpt-3.5-turbo和gpt-4相媲美的说法形成对比。我们仍需付出大量努力来生产更强的开源LLM以服务于智能体目的。

items.capEn: Figure 3: AgentBench OA scores with regard to all tested OSS LLMs.
items.capZh: 图3：所有测试的开源LLM的AgentBench总体得分。

items.en: In the evaluation, we analyze some important factors that impact an LLM agent’s performance on AgentBench, including outcome portion analysis, code training, and the difference between API-based commercial LLMs and OSS LLM competitors. More insights and case studies into the ability of planning, self-correction, and tool use are provided in Appendix J.2.
items.zh: 在评估中，我们分析了影响LLM智能体在AgentBench上性能的一些重要因素，包括结果类型分析、代码训练以及基于API的商业LLM与开源LLM竞争者之间的差异。关于规划、自我纠正和工具使用能力的更多见解和案例研究见附录J.2。

items.en: Portion of Different Types of Execution Outcomes. We report ratios of different types of execution outcomes (Cf. Section 2 for introduction) in Table 4. It is Task Limit Exceeded that dominantly caused the incompleteness of AgentBench tasks. It means that despite the instruction following of most LLM agents, they fail to solve the challenge in given time or fall into repeated generation when interaction turns grow up, indicating weak reasoning and decision-making abilities.
items.zh: 不同类型执行结果的比例。我们在表4中报告了不同类型执行结果的比例（参见第2节介绍）。任务限制超时是导致AgentBench任务未完成的主要原因。这意味着尽管大多数LLM智能体遵循指令，但它们未能在给定时间内解决挑战，或者在交互轮次增加时陷入重复生成，表明推理和决策能力薄弱。

items.en: In DB and DCG, LLM agents majorly encountered Invalid Format errors, meaning they do not correctly follow the instruction’s format requirements. The format verification is stringent for DB, and no retry opportunities are provided. Furthermore, the task’s expected output may be close to certain models’ training data, yet not precisely aligned with. This discrepancy can lead the models to revert to their pre-trained formatting, inadvertently overlooking the specific requirements we provide. (Cf. Appendix J.2.1) For DCG, its instruction could be longer and more complicated than other tasks due to the need to introduce game rules, making some LLMs feel confused. In HH and WB, another major issue is about Invalid Action, where LLM agents generate actions beyond predefined action spaces. These two tasks provide many discrete action options at each turn, and many LLMs fail to generate an action from them and, therefore, cause errors. For specific ratios of each LLM, please refer to Appendix J.1.
items.zh: 在DB和DCG中，LLM智能体主要遇到无效格式错误，意味着它们没有正确遵循指令的格式要求。DB的格式验证严格，且没有重试机会。此外，任务的预期输出可能接近某些模型的训练数据，但并未精确对齐。这种差异可能导致模型恢复到其预训练格式，无意中忽略了我们提供的具体要求。（参见附录J.2.1）对于DCG，由于其需要介绍游戏规则，指令可能比其他任务更长更复杂，使一些LLM感到困惑。在HH和WB中，另一个主要问题是无效动作，即LLM智能体生成超出预定义动作空间的动作。这两个任务在每一轮提供许多离散动作选项，许多LLM未能从中生成动作，从而导致错误。关于每个LLM的具体比例，请参考附录J.1。

items.en: Impact of Code Training. We find that code tuning might deeply influence a model’s way of inferential generation and thinking, even beyond topics just about coding. From the comparison of codellama and llama-2 series, tuning with code seems to give models an edge in tasks that follow a relatively static procedure (e.g., Web Shopping). But, this kind of tuning might also affect the model’s general thinking ability, as codellama series does not perform as well in the Digital Card Game as llama-2 series. This points to a balance between being good at following procedures and being good at general thinking when tuning LLMs.
items.zh: 代码训练的影响。我们发现代码调优可能深刻影响模型的推理生成和思维方式，甚至超越编码主题。从codellama和llama-2系列的比较来看，使用代码进行调优似乎使模型在遵循相对静态程序的任务（例如Web Shopping）中具有优势。但是，这种调优也可能影响模型的通用思维能力，因为codellama系列在数字卡牌游戏中的表现不如llama-2系列。这表明在调优LLM时，需要在擅长遵循程序和擅长通用思维之间取得平衡。

items.en: Impact of High-Quality Alignment Data Training. Another helpful comparison would be between vicuna-13b and llama-2-13b. While they share the same base LLM, vicuna-13b is aligned by training on ShareGPT’s data (generated by gpt-4 and gpt-3.5-turbo, shared by users) and llama-2-13b is aligned from scratch. As a result, vicuna-13b outperforms llama-2-13b on AgentBench, and even performs comparably to 3 times larger codellama-34b. This indicates that high-quality alignment is still a key to develop better LLM agents.
items.zh: 高质量对齐数据训练的影响。另一个有用的比较是vicuna-13b和llama-2-13b。虽然它们共享相同的基础LLM，但vicuna-13b通过训练ShareGPT的数据（由gpt-4和gpt-3.5-turbo生成，由用户共享）进行对齐，而llama-2-13b从头开始对齐。结果，vicuna-13b在AgentBench上优于llama-2-13b，甚至与3倍大的codellama-34b表现相当。这表明高质量对齐仍然是开发更好LLM智能体的关键。

items.en: Unexpected Similar Performance of llama-2-13b and llama-2-70b. During our experiments, we were surprised to find that llama-2-13b and llama-2-70b perform similarly despite the significant gap between their sizes. After carefully checking and re-running experiments, the results are unchanged. We think that it indicates llama-2-70b’s insufficient pre-training. While both llama-2-13b and llama-2-70b are pre-trained with 2T tokens, a larger LLM should be trained with more tokens according to the scaling law (Hoffmann et al., 2022).
items.zh: llama-2-13b和llama-2-70b的意外相似性能。在我们的实验中，我们惊讶地发现llama-2-13b和llama-2-70b尽管规模差距显著，但表现相似。经过仔细检查和重新运行实验，结果没有变化。我们认为这表明llama-2-70b的预训练不足。虽然llama-2-13b和llama-2-70b都使用2T个token进行预训练，但根据缩放定律（Hoffmann等人，2022），更大的LLM应该用更多的token进行训练。

items.en: Evaluation of LLMs. The general capabilities of self-supervised (Liu et al., 2021) LLMs (Brown et al., 2020; Chowdhery et al., 2022; Zhang et al., 2022; Scao et al., 2022; Zeng et al., 2022; Touvron et al., 2023), especially those chat-aligned ones (Ouyang et al., 2022; Anthropic, 2023a; OpenAI, 2023), have refreshed people’s impression on deep learning systems and significantly transcended the conventional scope of NLP evaluation. It thus makes the evaluation of LLMs an urgent and challenging problem. Compared to previous efforts focusing on a subset of specified tasks (Wang et al., 2019; ; Gehrmann et al., 2021), an increasing number of benchmarks are including broader spectra of tasks and datasets (Hendrycks et al., 2021b; Liang et al., 2022; Srivastava et al., 2023) in the evaluation. However, most of them are still limited to traditional tasks and thus fail to evaluate LLMs’ open-ended generation, multi-turn interaction, and ability to act as agents.
items.zh: LLM的评估。自监督（Liu等人，2021）LLM（Brown等人，2020；Chowdhery等人，2022；Zhang等人，2022；Scao等人，2022；Zeng等人，2022；Touvron等人，2023），尤其是那些经过聊天对齐的模型（Ouyang等人，2022；Anthropic，2023a；OpenAI，2023）的通用能力，刷新了人们对深度学习系统的印象，并显著超越了NLP评估的传统范围。这使得LLM的评估成为一个紧迫且具有挑战性的问题。与以往专注于特定任务子集的工作（Wang等人，2019；Gehrmann等人，2021）相比，越来越多的基准测试在评估中包含了更广泛的任务和数据集（Hendrycks等人，2021b；Liang等人，2022；Srivastava等人，2023）。然而，其中大多数仍然局限于传统任务，因此无法评估LLM的开放式生成、多轮交互以及作为智能体的能力。

items.en: LLM-as-Agent. In pre-LLM era, text game environments such as TextWorld (Côté et al., 2019), Jericho (Hausknecht et al., 2020), and LIGHT (Urbanek et al., 2019) are dominant in language agent study which bases on BERT (Devlin et al., 2019) and reinforcement learning. With the advent of LLMs, the study of LLM agents begins to thrive (Huang et al., 2022), especially after Chain-of-Thought (Wei et al., 2022b) came out. ReAct (Yao et al., 2023b) is a pioneer work to combine CoT reasoning and actions in agent tasks. Later, a bunch of advanced reasoning strategies (Kim et al., 2023; Shinn et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2023d; Liu et al., 2023; Yao et al., 2023a; Gu et al., 2023) and applications (Park et al., 2023; Richards, 2023; Nakajima, 2023; age, 2023) for LLM-as-Agent have emerged and arouse much public interest. Nevertheless, limited datasets and models and available on the topic, without a standard and comprehensive benchmark. AgentBench presents the first systematic benchmark for evaluating LLM-as-Agent with a broad coverage of tasks and available LLMs. Additionally, it also initiates the idea of adopting agent tasks to measure LLM performance.
items.zh: LLM作为智能体。在LLM之前的时代，基于BERT（Devlin等人，2019）和强化学习的文本游戏环境，如TextWorld（Côté等人，2019）、Jericho（Hausknecht等人，2020）和LIGHT（Urbanek等人，2019），在语言智能体研究中占主导地位。随着LLM的出现，LLM智能体的研究开始蓬勃发展（Huang等人，2022），尤其是在思维链（Wei等人，2022b）出现之后。ReAct（Yao等人，2023b）是将CoT推理和动作结合到智能体任务中的先驱工作。后来，一系列针对LLM作为智能体的高级推理策略（Kim等人，2023；Shinn等人，2023；Wang等人，2023d；Liu等人，2023；Yao等人，2023a；Gu等人，2023）和应用（Park等人，2023；Richards，2023；Nakajima，2023；age，2023）出现并引起了公众的广泛兴趣。然而，关于该主题的数据集和模型有限，缺乏标准且全面的基准测试。AgentBench提出了第一个系统性的基准测试，用于评估LLM作为智能体的能力，涵盖了广泛的任务和可用的LLM。此外，它还开创了采用智能体任务来衡量LLM性能的想法。

items.en: Evaluating LLMs in Executive Environments. As LLMs become increasingly capable of real-world challenges, there is also a trend to evaluate them in executive environments rather than static datasets. Besides text games (e.g., ALFWorld (Shridhar et al., 2020b)), another main stream of works lies in code execution. APPS (Hendrycks et al., 2021a), HumanEval (Chen et al., 2021) and MBPP (Austin et al., 2021) pioneer the effort to evaluate code LLMs for functional correctness instead of text similarity. The paradigm has been later widely recognized and adopted in following works (Li et al., 2022; Zheng et al., 2023; Nijkamp et al., 2023). However, few previous code evaluation frameworks consider multi-turn interactions. A concurrent work InterCode (Yang et al., 2023) releases a framework that allows evaluation of interaction between models and Bash and SQL environments, which are similar to OS and DB tasks in AgentBench.
items.zh: 在执行环境中评估LLM。随着LLM越来越能够应对现实世界的挑战，也有一种趋势是在执行环境中评估它们，而不是使用静态数据集。除了文本游戏（例如ALFWorld（Shridhar等人，2020b）），另一个主要工作方向是代码执行。APPS（Hendrycks等人，2021a）、HumanEval（Chen等人，2021）和MBPP（Austin等人，2021）开创了评估代码LLM功能正确性而非文本相似性的工作。该范式后来在后续工作中得到广泛认可和采用（Li等人，2022；Zheng等人，2023；Nijkamp等人，2023）。然而，很少有先前的代码评估框架考虑多轮交互。一项并行工作InterCode（Yang等人，2023）发布了一个框架，允许评估模型与Bash和SQL环境之间的交互，这些环境类似于AgentBench中的OS和DB任务。

items.en: We present AgentBench, a systematically designed multi-dimensional evolving benchmark for evaluating LLMs as agents. For the first time, we include such a wide array of up to 8 real-world challenges to evaluate LLM agents, and establish a unified testing framework and toolkit for agile evaluation. An extensive study of 27 LLMs, including API-based and Open-sourced, is carefully conducted in a standard setting. In our assessment, contemporary commercial models have demonstrated preliminary capabilities as agents in analysis, planning, execution of plans, tool invocation, and self-reflection. These abilities suggest their nascent proficiency in addressing real-world challenges. Conversely, we posit that open-source models might either lack some of these competencies or, at best, possess only a subset of them simultaneously. We expect AgentBench to serve as a cornerstone for later study to develop better and more applicable intelligent LLM agents.
items.zh: 我们提出了AgentBench，一个系统设计的多维演化基准测试，用于评估LLM作为智能体。我们首次包含了多达8个现实世界挑战的广泛阵列来评估LLM智能体，并建立了一个统一的测试框架和工具包以实现敏捷评估。在标准设置下，我们对27个LLM（包括基于API和开源的）进行了广泛研究。在我们的评估中，当代商业模型已初步展现出作为智能体的能力，包括分析、规划、执行计划、工具调用和自我反思。这些能力表明它们在应对现实世界挑战方面具有初步的熟练度。相反，我们认为开源模型可能缺乏这些能力中的某些部分，或者最多只能同时拥有其中的一部分。我们期望AgentBench能为后续研究奠定基础，以开发更好、更适用的智能LLM智能体。
